repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

`buf` is optional even though its needed to perform conversions and the ordering of `(buf, row, col)` is not consistent.

## Solution

make `buf` mandatory on `vim.range` and `vim.pos` and enforce the `buf, row, col` ordering

Addresses point 10 and 5 of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38561

@justinmk @ofseed

comment_data.body: another potential consistency improvement: why do we accept `pos:[row, col]` list here, but `Pos:extmark()` expects `row, col` positional parameters?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4250,41 +4250,35 @@ by |vim.Pos| objects.
     Fields: ~
       • {row}         (`integer`) 0-based byte index.
       • {col}         (`integer`) 0-based byte index.
-      • {buf}?        (`integer`) Optional buffer handle.
-
-                      When specified, it indicates that this position belongs
-                      to a specific buffer. This field is required when
-                      performing position conversions.
+      • {buf}         (`integer`) buffer handle.
       • {to_lsp}      (`fun(pos: vim.Pos, position_encoding: lsp.PositionEncodingKind)`)
                       See |Pos:to_lsp()|.
       • {lsp}         (`fun(buf: integer, pos: lsp.Position, position_encoding: lsp.PositionEncodingKind)`)
                       See |Pos:lsp()|.
       • {to_cursor}   (`fun(pos: vim.Pos): integer, integer`) See
                       |Pos:to_cursor()|.
-      • {cursor}      (`fun(pos: [integer, integer], opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
-                      See |Pos:cursor()|.
+      • {cursor}      (`fun(buf: integer, pos: [integer, integer])`) See
+                      |Pos:cursor()|.
       • {to_extmark}  (`fun(pos: vim.Pos): integer, integer`) See
                       |Pos:to_extmark()|.
-      • {extmark}     (`fun(row: integer, col: integer, opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
-                      See |Pos:extmark()|.
+      • {extmark}     (`fun(buf: integer, row: integer, col: integer)`) See
+                      |Pos:extmark()|.
 
 
-Pos:cursor({pos}, {opts})                                       *Pos:cursor()*
+Pos:cursor({buf}, {pos})                                        *Pos:cursor()*

comment_data.body: same here: start_pos/end_pos are lists, but `exmark:fun()` above expects 4 positional params.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4402,14 +4390,14 @@ Provides operations to compare, calculate, and convert ranges represented by
       • {lsp}         (`fun(buf: integer, range: lsp.Range, position_encoding: lsp.PositionEncodingKind)`)
                       See |Range:lsp()|.
       • {to_extmark}  (`fun(range: vim.Range)`) See |Range:to_extmark()|.
-      • {extmark}     (`fun(start_row: integer, start_col: integer, end_row: integer, end_col: integer, opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
+      • {extmark}     (`fun(buf: integer, start_row: integer, start_col: integer, end_row: integer, end_col: integer)`)
                       See |Range:extmark()|.
       • {to_cursor}   (`fun(range: vim.Range)`) See |Range:to_cursor()|.
-      • {cursor}      (`fun(buf: integer, start_pos: [integer, integer], end_pos: [integer, integer], opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
+      • {cursor}      (`fun(buf: integer, start_pos: [integer, integer], end_pos: [integer, integer])`)

comment_data.body:
are there any complications if we support `vim.pos(0,...)` to mean "current buffer"? 

we'll also need to think about "current window" (`opts.win` I guess).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -303,7 +303,8 @@ api.nvim_set_decoration_provider(document_color_ns, {
 local function get_hl_info_under_cursor(provider)
   local cursor_row, cursor_col = unpack(api.nvim_win_get_cursor(0)) --- @type integer, integer
   cursor_row = cursor_row - 1 -- Convert to 0-based index
-  local cursor_pos = vim.pos(cursor_row, cursor_col)
+  local buf = api.nvim_get_current_buf()
+  local cursor_pos = vim.pos(buf, cursor_row, cursor_col)

comment_data.body: My reasoning was that "cursor-like" APIs like `nvim_win_get_cursor()`/`nvim_win_set_cursor()` use a `{row, col}` tuple, but "extmark-like" APIs like `nvim_buf_get_text()`/`nvim_buf_get_extmarks()` use raw integers. So, the current implementation would maintain consistency with those APIs, just like `Pos:lsp()` accepts an "LSP-like" position as input
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4250,41 +4250,35 @@ by |vim.Pos| objects.
     Fields: ~
       • {row}         (`integer`) 0-based byte index.
       • {col}         (`integer`) 0-based byte index.
-      • {buf}?        (`integer`) Optional buffer handle.
-
-                      When specified, it indicates that this position belongs
-                      to a specific buffer. This field is required when
-                      performing position conversions.
+      • {buf}         (`integer`) buffer handle.
       • {to_lsp}      (`fun(pos: vim.Pos, position_encoding: lsp.PositionEncodingKind)`)
                       See |Pos:to_lsp()|.
       • {lsp}         (`fun(buf: integer, pos: lsp.Position, position_encoding: lsp.PositionEncodingKind)`)
                       See |Pos:lsp()|.
       • {to_cursor}   (`fun(pos: vim.Pos): integer, integer`) See
                       |Pos:to_cursor()|.
-      • {cursor}      (`fun(pos: [integer, integer], opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
-                      See |Pos:cursor()|.
+      • {cursor}      (`fun(buf: integer, pos: [integer, integer])`) See
+                      |Pos:cursor()|.
       • {to_extmark}  (`fun(pos: vim.Pos): integer, integer`) See
                       |Pos:to_extmark()|.
-      • {extmark}     (`fun(row: integer, col: integer, opts: vim.Pos.Optional?)`)
-                      See |Pos:extmark()|.
+      • {extmark}     (`fun(buf: integer, row: integer, col: integer)`) See
+                      |Pos:extmark()|.
 
 
-Pos:cursor({pos}, {opts})                                       *Pos:cursor()*
+Pos:cursor({buf}, {pos})                                        *Pos:cursor()*

comment_regular_data.body:
🥲 I personally use it as a simple utility to work with ranges as digits (with `vim.treesitter.get_string_parser`):

```lua
local inter = vim.range(0,0,0,0):intersect(vim.range(0,0,1,0)) -- error
```

However, I get an error somewhere in my code because it checks the buffer length, so the change is for the better. I hope that in the future there will be a way to work with ranges without requiring a buffer.

P.S. Nvm, `local Range = require("vim.treesitter._range")`.

> I hope that in the future there will be a way to work with ranges without requiring a buffer.

@drowning-cat thanks for reviewing/feedback. We will plan to support "unknown buffer" use-cases, with `buf=-1` or something like that.

> Nvm, `local Range = require("vim.treesitter._range")`.

that's an internal/unsupported pattern, just to be clear.

title: feat!: make `buf` mandatory always on `vim.range` and `vim.pos`
